framing_signals_checked: Does the engineering layer claim the Gita is really a tech manual? No — the verse explicitly frames itself as 'an additional layer atop the verse, not a replacement,' consistent with the project's stated thesis.

Does it claim Krishna was 'really talking about engineering'? No — the iteration log explicitly maps the verse's literal kṣatriya frame to its engineering analog while preserving the literal as primary.

Does it project modern frames backward? It uses modern engineering scenarios as analogs but does not claim the Sanskrit anticipates them. The doctrinal claim (svadharma is role-bound) generalises; the kṣatriya-specific instance does not collapse into the generalisation.
verdict_reason: No tech-manual framing. The engineering layer is an analog, not a substitution. PASS.
